articles.title: US announces sweeping new semiconductor export controls targeting China
articles.description: The Biden administration unveiled expanded restrictions on advanced chip exports to China, affecting major suppliers including NVIDIA and ASML. Markets dropped sharply on the news.

articles.title: ECB signals rate cut in June as eurozone PMI weakens further
articles.description: ECB President Lagarde indicated the central bank is ready to ease monetary policy at its June meeting, citing weaker-than-expected manufacturing data across the eurozone.

articles.title: OPEC+ delays production increase to Q3, Brent crude jumps 2.1%
articles.description: Saudi Arabia pushed for continued output restraint amid uncertain demand outlook. Oil prices surged as markets adjusted expectations for global supply.

articles.title: India and EU sign landmark free trade agreement after 16 years
articles.description: The comprehensive trade deal covers tariff reductions on over 90% of goods and sets new standards for digital trade and sustainability provisions.

articles.title: Taiwan conducts largest military exercise since 2022 as Beijing escalates rhetoric
articles.description: The five-day drill simulates defense against a naval blockade. China's foreign ministry condemned the exercise. Semiconductor stocks fell across Asian markets.

articles.title: Norges Bank holds key rate at 4.25%, signals cut in September
articles.description: The central bank kept rates unchanged but adjusted forward guidance, suggesting the easing cycle will begin later this year if inflation continues to moderate.

articles.title: South Korea snap election called after president's removal from office
articles.description: The constitutional court upheld the impeachment, triggering a 60-day countdown to new elections. Markets reacted calmly as the transition was widely expected.

articles.title: Russia-Ukraine front lines see renewed heavy fighting near Pokrovsk
articles.description: Both sides reported significant casualties as Russian forces launched a new offensive. EU foreign ministers are meeting to discuss additional military aid packages.

articles.title: Lithium prices spike 15% on Chile export quota announcement
articles.description: Chile's government announced quarterly production caps on lithium exports, raising concerns about EV battery supply chains globally.
